topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the embossed lettering on the acceptance letter, a slow, deliberate movement that sent a pleasant warmth spreading through her chest. The sunlight streaming through the library window caught the gold foil, making the paper shimmer. She had worked so hard for this—the late nights studying, the relentless pursuit of excellence—and now, this scholarship felt like the culmination of everything. A wide, unguarded smile stretched across her face as she reread the paragraph detailing the financial support. Then, a single word snagged her attention: "Recipient: Julian Vance." Elara blinked, the warmth instantly curdling into a cold, sharp shock. She reread the entire document, her breath catching in her throat. It wasn't hers.

[story 2]

"This is incredible," Marcus declared, spinning in his chair, his eyes alight with pure exhilaration as he held the official notification. He bounced on the balls of his feet, a restless energy vibrating off him. He was practically buzzing, ready to celebrate the achievement. But as he scanned the fine print, the energy drained from his posture, replaced by a sudden, heavy stillness. The names listed in the award details didn't match his own application. A strange, hollow sensation settled in his stomach, like a stone dropped into still water. He felt his cheeks flush hot, a sudden, unwelcome tightness constricting his throat.

[story 3]

The air in the common room felt electric; a low hum of shared excitement filled the space as Liam recounted his triumphs to his friends. He leaned forward, his expression open and eager, every gesture animated with buoyant confidence. He was soaring, convinced this recognition was entirely his due. When he finally pulled up the official documentation on his laptop, the color seemed to leach from the screen. The carefully constructed buoyancy vanished. His fingers froze over the keyboard, the triumphant smile dissolving into a slack-jawed expression. A deep, unsettling chill crept up his spine, making him instinctively pull his sweater tighter around himself.
